name: My Favourite Pie Crust Recipe
recipeInstructions:
Do not use shortening. (This is a slight variation from the pie dough that I have made all my life—this version is a definite keeper for me. It yields 6 to 8-9 inch pie shells (tins), or you can make mini pies by using a pie maker. The remaining pastry can be stored covered in the refrigerator up to two weeks, or frozen for longer storage.)
1. Measure flour, sugar, salt, and baking powder into a large bowl.
2. Cut in lard until the mixture is the size of tiny peas. Work with your hands for easier cutting.
3. Break egg into a measuring cup and beat with a fork until frothy. Add vinegar to the egg mixture.
4. Mix enough cold water in the same cup to equal 1 cup, then pour it over the flour mixture while continuously working the dough with your hands. Keep adding until all liquid is absorbed.
5. Divide the dough into four balls, wrap each ball in plastic, and refrigerate for up to two weeks or freeze for longer storage.
6. If the dough is too moist to work with, add a small amount of flour. There will also be extra flour on your work surface for rolling out the pastry.
